nodes.x: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend to know understanding.

Listen to Learn By Elder Russell M. Nelson Of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles In his invocation for this session of conference, Elder Hugh W. Pinnock prayed that we might listen carefully. Many articles in Church literature have dealt with the important art of listening. They support a proverb that teaches this vital lesson: “Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise” (…

putting in practice, the means of remedying them. If parents will follow this course through life, and bring their children up in the belief and practice of the principles of life, and teach them to know themselves, the end of their creation, and to keep diligently the commandments of the Lord, they will grow up far superior to their parents in body and mind. When this is done we may expect, if…
To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;

Teaching Our Children to Understand By Cheryl A. Esplin Second Counselor in the Primary General Presidency Teaching our children to understand is more than just imparting information. It’s helping our children get the doctrine into their hearts. As years pass, many details in my life are becoming more and more dim, but some of the memories that remain most clear are the births of each of our…
For I give you good doctrine, forsake ye not my law.
For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.
Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye.
For verily I say unto you, my law shall be kept on this land.
I will keep thy statutes: O forsake me not utterly.
Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, I will declare unto you my doctrine.
For I was my father’s son, tender and only beloved in the sight of my mother.
Our lonely moments, with a smile! Beloved she lived, beloved she died. Her fathers joy and mothers pride — Beloved by all who did her see,
I soon discovered among the mothers, a kind of carelessness with regard to their children, even when their lives were in danger. So I called them together, and endeavored to impress upon their
(For from my youth he was brought up with me, as with a father, and I have guided her from my mother’s womb;)

'With Father's return, the happy Mother conscientiously shared with
him the difficult and important business of bringing up their Son; and
both in union worked highly beneficially for his spiritual
development. The practical and rigorous Father directed his chief aim
to developing the Boy's intellect and character; the mild, pious,
poetic-minded Mother, on the other hand, strove for the…
He taught me also, and said unto me, Let thine heart retain my words: keep my commandments, and live.
And thou shalt speak unto him, and put words in his mouth: and I will be with thy mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do.
And he said: Yea, I will keep thy commandments with all my heart.
And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.
Therefore I give unto you a commandment, to teach these things freely unto your children, saying:
And I give unto you a commandment that then ye shall teach them unto all men; for they shall be taught unto all nations, kindreds, tongues and people.
Get wisdom, get understanding: forget it not; neither decline from the words of my mouth.
Forsake the foolish, and live; and go in the way of understanding.
Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
O ye simple, understand wisdom: and, ye fools, be ye of an understanding heart.
For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
Understanding is a wellspring of life unto him that hath it: but the instruction of fools is folly.
Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee.
Preserve my soul; for I am holy: O thou my God, save thy servant that trusteth in thee.
Discretion shall preserve thee, understanding shall keep thee:

And, behold, I am with thee, and will keep thee in all places whither thou goest, and will bring thee again into this land; for I will not leave thee, until I have done that which I have spoken to thee of.

And she shall put the raiment of her captivity from off her, and shall remain in thine house, and bewail her father and her mother a full month: and after that thou shalt go in unto her, and be her husband, and she shall be thy wife.
The Lord shall preserve thee from all evil: he shall preserve thy soul.
Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.
Exalt her, and she shall promote thee: she shall bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her.
Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee.
¶ Therefore, behold, I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak comfortably unto her.
Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.
For I will promote thee unto very great honour, and I will do whatsoever thou sayest unto me: come therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people.
She shall give to thine head an ornament of grace: a crown of glory shall she deliver to thee.
She shall give to thine head an ornament of grace: a crown of glory shall she deliver to thee.
For I will send into her pestilence, and blood into her streets; and the wounded shall be judged in the midst of her by the sword upon her on every side; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
For they shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck.
Exalt her, and she shall promote thee: she shall bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her.
Therefore hell hath enlarged herself, and opened her mouth without measure: and their glory, and their multitude, and their pomp, and he that rejoiceth, shall descend into it.
Therefore, hell hath enlarged herself, and opened her mouth without measure; and their glory, and their multitude, and their pomp, and he that rejoiceth, shall descend into it.
Hear, O my son, and receive my sayings; and the years of thy life shall be many.
I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days: thy years are throughout all generations.
And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and believeth on him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day.
For behold, I say unto you that I have beheld that many generations shall pass away, and there shall be great wars and contentions among my people.
But, behold, my Beloved Son, which was my Beloved and Chosen from the beginning, said unto me—Father, thy will be done, and the glory be thine forever.
I have taught thee in the way of wisdom; I have led thee in right paths.
I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.
Teach me thy way, O Lord, and lead me in a plain path, because of mine enemies.
Teach me to do thy will; for thou art my God: thy spirit is good; lead me into the land of uprightness.
Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.
If not, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.
When thou goest, thy steps shall not be straitened; and when thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.
Then shalt thou walk in thy way safely, and thy foot shall not stumble.

O Lord, wilt thou encircle me around in the robe of thy righteousness! O Lord, wilt thou make a way for mine escape before mine enemies! Wilt thou make my path straight before me! Wilt thou not place a stumbling block in my way—but that thou wouldst clear my way before me, and hedge not up my way, but the ways of mine enemy.
Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks, and lookest narrowly unto all my paths; thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet.
When thou goest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and when thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.
Then shalt thou bring forth thy stuff by day in their sight, as stuff for removing: and thou shalt go forth at even in their sight, as they that go forth into captivity.
Take fast hold of instruction; let her not go: keep her; for she is thy life.
Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:

"I have not only released her from punishment, but I have even let her
go. I let her go, just as I once promised her, if she should ever again
presume to meet Tihamer Csorbai."
Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.
¶ Enter not into the path of the wicked, and go not in the way of evil men.
Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.
Incline not my heart to any evil thing, to practise wicked works with men that work iniquity: and let me not eat of their dainties.
¶ Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the Lord, and depart from evil.
Depart from evil, and do good; and dwell for evermore.
Avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it, and pass away.

find that their hidden works of darkness will not avail them when the Lord feels after them, and when His condemnation rests upon them; they will find this out to their everlasting sorrow. Men who are guilty of these practices, and who seek to lead away the unwary, and to prey upon the ignorant and unsophisticated, and to take advantage of their fears, and instil superstition into the mind,…
Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
Dig thou through the wall in their sight, and carry out thereby.
And it shall come to pass, if they are not more faithful unto me, it shall be taken away, even that which they have.
For they sleep not, except they have done mischief; and their sleep is taken away, unless they cause some to fall.
For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night.
Woe to them that devise iniquity, and work evil upon their beds! when the morning is light, they practise it, because it is in the power of their hand.
None shall slumber nor sleep; neither shall the girdle of their loins be loosed, nor the latchet of their shoes be broken;
Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are perished.
For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.
Let death seize upon them, and let them go down quick into hell: for wickedness is in their dwellings, and among them.
The robbery of the wicked shall destroy them; because they refuse to do judgment.
And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and unleavened bread; and with bitter herbs they shall eat it.
Lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the judgment of any of the afflicted.
And thus they might murder, and plunder, and steal, and commit whoredoms and all manner of wickedness, contrary to the laws of their country and also the laws of their God.
But the path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day.
That which is of God is light; and he that receiveth light, and continueth in God, receiveth more light; and that light groweth brighter and brighter until the perfect day.
brighter and brighter until the perfect day. God bless you and lead you in the path of life, in the name of Jesus. Amen.
The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.
The wicked are estranged from the womb: they go astray as soon as they be born, speaking lies.
Lo, their good is not in their hand: the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the Lord: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.
There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the weary be at rest.
¶ My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.
My son, attend unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my understanding:
¶ Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.
Moreover he said unto me, Son of man, all my words that I shall speak unto thee receive in thine heart, and hear with thine ears.
¶ Rise up, ye women that are at ease; hear my voice, ye careless daughters; give ear unto my speech.
So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.
¶ My son, let not them depart from thine eyes: keep sound wisdom and discretion:
But thine eyes and thine heart are not but for thy covetousness, and for to shed innocent blood, and for oppression, and for violence, to do it.
Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck.
Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.
For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.
All they that be fat upon earth shall eat and worship: all they that go down to the dust shall bow before him: and none can keep alive his own soul.
Their soul abhorreth all manner of meat; and they draw near unto the gates of death.
But blessed are they who are faithful and endure, whether in life or in death, for they shall inherit eternal life.
And one of you say unto them, Depart in peace, be ye warmed and filled; notwithstanding ye give them not those things which are needful to the body; what doth it profit?
¶ Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
Hear thou, my son, and be wise, and guide thine heart in the way.
¶ Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
¶ When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul;
Incline my heart unto thy testimonies, and not to covetousness.
Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee.
The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardness.
For it is a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee; they shall withal be fitted in thy lips.
Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.
He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction.
Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.
Now, my God, let, I beseech thee, thine eyes be open, and let thine ears be attent unto the prayer that is made in this place.
Let my sentence come forth from thy presence; let thine eyes behold the things that are equal.
Give not sleep to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids.
So that thou shalt be mad for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see.
That thine eyes may be open unto the supplication of thy servant, and unto the supplication of thy people Israel, to hearken unto them in all that they call for unto thee.
Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.
Thus saith the Lord, Stand ye in the ways, and see, and ask for the old paths, where is the good way, and walk therein, and ye shall find rest for your souls. But they said, We will not walk therein.
Therefore, gird up thy loins for the work. Let thy feet be shod also, for thou art chosen, and thy path lieth among the mountains, and among many nations.
My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path:
Thy way is in the sea, and thy path in the great waters, and thy footsteps are not known.
Turn not to the right hand nor to the left: remove thy foot from evil.
I have refrained my feet from every evil way, that I might keep thy word.
But I say unto you, That ye resist not evil: but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
Keep thy foot when thou goest to the house of God, and be more ready to hear, than to give the sacrifice of fools: for they consider not that they do evil.
But I say unto you, that ye shall not resist evil, but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also;
The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.
